Internet Protocol version 4 (IPv4) is the most widely used type of IP and uses a 32-bit format (e.g., 192.168.1.1), providing about 4.3 billion addresses. 
Internet Protocol version 6 (IPv6), on the other hand, uses a 128-bit alphanumeric format (e.g., 2001:0db8...) to provide almost unlimited addresses.
